name: Trim The Compact Two-Stage PTX Wait-Sync Cadence On The 22016B Shared Surface

summary.name: Trim The Compact Two-Stage PTX Wait-Sync Cadence On The 22016B Shared Surface
summary.hypothesis: Now that grouped_rows and register pressure are back on the compact winning surface, the most actionable remaining tax is CTA handoff cadence itself. A narrow retime of wait_group and __syncthreads placement on the 22,016 B PTX loop should reduce barrier without reopening the stage-count or shared-memory regressions from the failed round-6 branch.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Barrier and CTA handoff overhead are the clearest remaining local bottlenecks on the accepted compact PTX base.
summary.risk: Moderate. This stays entirely on the compact two-stage PTX loop, but it still touches fragile synchronization semantics.
ranking_notes: This is the strongest next move because barrier is now the largest explicit local stall on the accepted compact PTX surface.
It preserves the winning 202-register and 22,016 B shared-memory footprint instead of reopening 3-stage family risk.
If it cannot move the 24.70 ms base, the local compact PTX surface is likely close to exhausted.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if the trim requires increasing shared memory above 22,016 B or materially increasing registers above the current 202-register budget.
expected_local_changes: Retune wait_group and __syncthreads placement inside the compact two-stage PTX hot-band loop.
Keep stage depth at 2 and shared memory at 22,016 B.
Do not change grouped_rows or launch geometry in the first pass.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
